Title: Wegner, Max
Subject Area: architecture (object genre)|Classical|iconography|music (discipline)|musical notation|scores (documents for music)|sculpture (visual works)
Overview: Scholar of classical sculpture, architecture, and musical iconography. Richard Brilliant characterizes Wegner among the group of art historians writing between World War I and World War II who followed the example of Aloïs Riegl in attempting to define late antique art as its own style and not simply a declined version of a greater era.
